title: Fix 'Actor not available' error on client creation

requirements.text: In useQueries.ts and useActor.ts, diagnose and fix the root cause of the 'Actor not available' error that surfaces on the New Client page (ClientDetailPage in create mode) when the user is authenticated via Internet Identity. The actor must be initialized and ready before any mutation (createClient) is attempted. If the actor is not yet ready, the page must show a loading state rather than an error banner. Once the actor is available, the createClient mutation must fire successfully when the Create button is pressed.

quotes: Actor not available error on creating client now during testing.

acceptanceCriteria: When a fully authenticated user navigates to /clients/new, no 'Actor not available' error banner is displayed.
The Create button is not disabled while the actor is initializing — the page shows a spinner or loading indicator instead.
Once the actor is ready, pressing Create with valid field values calls createClient on the backend actor and navigates back to /clients on success.
If the actor genuinely fails to initialize, a user-friendly error message is shown (not a raw 'Actor not available' string).

requirements.text: In useActor.ts, ensure the actor is created as soon as an authenticated identity is available and that React Query caches it so subsequent hook calls on the same page receive the same actor instance without re-triggering initialization. The actor query must not return undefined or null once the identity is confirmed; if initialization is in progress it must return a loading state, not an error state.

quotes: Actor not available error on creating client now during testing.

acceptanceCriteria: useActor returns a stable actor reference for the same identity without reinitialization on each render.
While the actor is being created, useActor returns { isLoading: true, actor: undefined } — no error is thrown.
After the actor is ready, isLoading is false and actor is the authenticated backend actor instance.
The actor instance is invalidated and recreated only when the identity changes (login/logout).

requirements.text: In ClientDetailPage.tsx, guard the createClient mutation call so it only fires when the actor is confirmed non-null. If the actor is not yet available when the Create button is pressed, show an inline loading indicator on the button rather than propagating an error. Wire the useCreateClient mutation from useQueries.ts so it receives the actor reference correctly and does not fall back to a no-op when the actor is undefined.

quotes: Actor not available error on creating client now during testing.

acceptanceCriteria: The Create button in new-client mode is never disabled due to actor unavailability after the user is authenticated.
Pressing Create when the actor is not yet ready shows a loading spinner on the button and retries once the actor is available, or displays a clear 'Please wait, connecting…' message.
When the actor is ready and the form has valid values, pressing Create calls actor.createClient with the correct argument shape and navigates to /clients on success.
Any error returned by the backend is surfaced as a visible error message in the form.

constraints: Do not modify files listed under frontend.immutablePaths (useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts, main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui).
Do not change the backend schema or add new backend endpoints.
The fix must not regress any previously working mutation (updateClient, deleteClient, createJob, etc.).
nonGoals: Adding new client fields or changing the client data model.
Changing backend role-based access control logic.
Modifying the invoice, calendar, or any other page not related to this bug.
